attribute_description: Represents the Technology Readiness Level on a 1–9 scale as defined by Nomenclature 4.2, indicating the maturity of a technology across different years.
Aggregated maturity classification derived from TRL. Updated per tech_year. Allowed values: Prototype, Emerging, Mature. See Nomenclature 4.2 for detailed definitions.

Overall energy conversion efficiency calculated as main output energy divided by main input energy. Mathematically bounded between 0 and 1 (exclusive of 0, inclusive of 1). Engineering definition requires accounting for primary energy inputs and outputs, with values derived from kWh (electricity, heat) or Lower Heating Value (LHV) for chemical fuels.

Ratio of the main output's energy content (LHV) to the main input's energy content (LHV) at theoretical maximum, with a value range of 0 < η ≤ 1. This dimensionless ratio is used to assess the efficiency of energy conversion processes. May be left blank if not applicable.
The expected operational lifetime of an asset in years, defined by engineering standards and accounting practices.
Fixed one-time capital expenditure independent of installed capacity. Per-project lump sum with a source currency of EUR. Value must be ≥ 0.

Specific capital expenditure per unit of installed power capacity (kW), representing the primary scaling parameter for ConvTech. This value must be greater than or equal to zero and is denominated in EUR. It is used to calculate total capital expenditure based on installed capacity and is derived from engineering and accounting data.
Fixed one-time operational expenditure independent of capacity, encompassing detailed operational mathematical boundaries, specific accounting line items, and engineering definitions.

attribute_name: Fixed Operational Expenditure Percentage Of Capital Expenditure
attribute_description: Represents the fixed annual operational expenditure as a percentage of total capital expenditure (CAPEX), ranging from 0% to 100%. This value is used in financial planning and engineering calculations to estimate ongoing operational costs relative to initial investment. Accounting lines include maintenance, utilities, and other fixed operational costs.

attribute_name: Annual Operational Expenditure Per Installed Capacity
attribute_description: Fixed annual operational expenditure per unit of installed capacity, excluding fuel costs. This value represents the non-negative annual cost in EUR per kilowatt of installed capacity, applicable for engineering calculations and accounting purposes.

Variable operational expenditure per unit of energy or mass output, excluding fuel costs. Measured in EUR per kWh or EUR per kg, representing non-negative values used for accounting and engineering calculations.
Characteristic operating temperature of the core process.

Defines the smallest assumed unit capacity for the technology, specifying operational mathematical boundaries, accounting line items, and engineering definitions. A value of 0 indicates no minimum constraint.
Qualitative uncertainty rating for the techno-economic parameters.
Technology-specific weighted average cost of capital for annualising capital expenditure, defining operational mathematical bounds, accounting lines, and engineering definitions.
The nameplate capacity serving as the basis for scaling, defining operational mathematical boundaries, accounting lines, and engineering specifications.
The type of energy or material carrier used in storage, such as electricity, hydrogen, or biomass. Referenced from the Carrier tab for standardized abbreviations.
Defines the smallest assumed storage capacity, specifying operational mathematical boundaries, accounting line items, and engineering definitions related to energy storage systems.

Maximum charging power as a dimensionless ratio representing the fraction of rated storage capacity (C-rate equivalent), defining operational limits for battery charging systems. Values range from 0 (no charging) to 1 (full capacity charging), inclusive. Used in energy storage system accounting and engineering specifications.

Maximum discharging power as a dimensionless ratio representing the fraction of rated storage capacity that can be discharged under operational conditions. Defines the upper bound of power output relative to total energy storage capacity, critical for energy storage system performance evaluation and capacity planning.

Represents the energy efficiency during charging, measured as a ratio between 0 and 1, where 0 indicates no efficiency and 1 indicates perfect efficiency. Includes operational parameters, accounting lines, and engineering specifications.

Represents the energy efficiency during discharging, measured as a ratio between 0 and 1. Defines the operational mathematical bounds, accounting lines, and engineering definitions related to discharge efficiency.

Minimum state of charge as a ratio between 0 and 1 (inclusive), representing the lowest permissible fraction of total energy capacity available in a battery system. Defines operational boundaries for charge preservation and system safety.

The maximum state of charge, represented as a fraction of the total battery capacity, defining the upper operational limit for energy storage. This value is used in capacity planning and ensures compliance with safety and performance standards.

Fractional energy loss per hour during standby (self-discharge rate), represented as a dimensionless quantity. This value is used in calculating energy efficiency and system performance. It is typically a small value, often negligible, and may be set to 0 when loss is considered insignificant.

Capital expenditure divided by installed storage capacity (kWh), representing the cost per unit of energy storage. Defines CAPEX allocation for storage systems, with engineering bounds ensuring non-negative values and alignment with EUR-based financial reporting.

Fixed annual operational expenditure per unit of storage capacity, measured in EUR per kWh of storage capacity per year. This value represents a non-negative fixed cost component used in financial modeling and should be consistent with engineering definitions of storage capacity units.

Measures the embedded carbon dioxide equivalent per kilogram of product for direct air capture systems, specifically sorbent-based systems with a capacity of 100ktCO2. This includes all operational and engineering factors related to carbon capture and storage processes.
